key_takeaways: GE Vernova raised full-year 2024 guidance across revenue (high end of $34B–$35B), adjusted EBITDA margin (5%–7%), and free cash flow ($1.3B–$1.7B), driven by outperformance in Power and Electrification segments.

Gas Power demand is accelerating with 30 heavy-duty gas turbines booked in H1 2024 (12 HAs), and management expects H2 2024 orders to exceed H1; supply chain investment and CapEx are being deployed to meet demand.

The Vineyard Wind blade event poses near-term offshore wind execution risk, with potential collection timing shifts from 2024 to 2025, though no engineering design flaw was identified and Dogger Bank installation continues.
risk_flags: Vineyard Wind turbine blade manufacturing deviation requires inspection of ~150 blades; risk of revenue/collection timing slippage from 2024 into 2025.
Onshore Wind order inflection remains uncertain as customers face permitting challenges and elevated interest rates; North America recovery timing unclear.
Supply chain constraints (castings, forgings) could limit Gas Power capacity expansion despite strong order momentum.
Two employee/contractor fatalities in June 2024 highlight ongoing safety execution risks.
Offshore Wind segment remains loss-making; further blade or quality events could increase costs and delay project completions.
